title: Vibe-driven music generation via ElevenLabs Music API
description:
Add a /music command and MCP tool that generates short ambient tracks based on the current session vibe. Uses the ElevenLabs Music API (elevenlabs.music.compose) to synthesize music from a text prompt derived from vibe_tags and vibe mood. The generated track plays via the existing playback queue.

Key design points:
- New MCP tool: music(prompt, duration_ms, ephemeral) — generate and play a short track
- New /music command: /music [prompt] — generate from explicit prompt, or derive from current vibe if no prompt given
- Vibe-to-prompt mapping: translate vibe_tags like [warm] [satisfied] into music prompt descriptions (genre, tempo, mood)
- Default duration: short ambient clips (10-30s) suitable for background/transition moments
- ElevenLabs Music API: elevenlabs.music.compose(prompt=..., music_length_ms=...) returns streaming MP3 chunks
- Requires ElevenLabs paid plan (same API key as TTS)
- Output: ephemeral by default (.tts/ dir), or saved to output dir
- Playback via existing flock-serialized enqueue()

Multi-session safety:
- New config field: music: y|n (default n) in .tts/config.md
- New /music on|off command to toggle
- Only one session can play music at a time — music is sustained audio, not short chimes
- Use a system-wide flock (e.g. ~/.punt-tts/music.lock) to ensure exclusive playback
- If another session holds the lock, skip music silently (log debug)
- Chimes and speech continue to work normally across sessions (short, serialized)
- /music on in one session implicitly means other sessions won't play music until it releases

Reference: https://elevenlabs.io/docs/eleven-api/guides/cookbooks/music/quickstart

title: Screen reader mode: vocalize any plugin tool output
description:
Screen reader mode for Claude Code sessions. When voice is enabled, a PostToolUse hook vocalizes MCP tool output from any plugin — not just tts tools. Acts like a screen reader: speaks whatever text appears in tool results using the session default voice.

Mechanism: PostToolUse hook matches MCP tool results (configurable allowlist/blocklist). Extracts text content and instructs Claude to call speak with ephemeral=true.

Scope: Generic — no per-tool voices, no creative pacing. Uses session default voice for everything. Biff tools excluded from allowlist when biff voice mode is active (avoids double-speaking).

Complementary to tts-51v (continuous notify). Screen reader fires synchronously after each tool call. Continuous mode fires asynchronously during long operations when the model is busy and hooks can't fire.

Config: allowlist/blocklist in .tts/config.md to control which tools get vocalized.
close_reason: Wrong integration architecture. Per tiered protocol, plugins that want speech should reach into vox (L0+L1+L3), not vox reaching into every plugin's output via PostToolUse. See .tmp/integration-architecture.html.

title: Per-signal chime assets for continuous mode
description:
chime_done.mp3 is overloaded — it plays for task completion AND all 6 watcher signals (tests-pass, tests-fail, lint-pass, lint-fail, git-push-ok, merge-conflict). You can't distinguish success from failure by sound alone.

Step 1: Create distinct chime assets for each signal type:
- chime_done.mp3 — task completion (existing, keep as-is)
- chime_prompt.mp3 — permission/idle prompt (existing, keep as-is)
- chime_tests_pass.mp3 — bright, ascending
- chime_tests_fail.mp3 — low, descending
- chime_lint_pass.mp3 — light, crisp
- chime_lint_fail.mp3 — dull thud
- chime_git_push_ok.mp3 — triumphant, resolved
- chime_merge_conflict.mp3 — dissonant, alert

Step 2: Wire signal-to-chime mapping:
- watcher.py: _announce_chime() takes signal name, resolves to correct asset
- resolve_chime_path() becomes resolve_chime_path(signal) with per-signal lookup
- Fallback: if a signal-specific chime is missing, play chime_done.mp3

Asset generation options:
- Generate via ElevenLabs Music API (short 1-2s clips)
- Source from royalty-free sound libraries
- Synthesize programmatically (pydub tone generation)

This is a prerequisite for tts-9lr (vibe-driven chime selection), which layers mood modulation on top of the per-signal base chimes.
close_reason: Closed

title: Adopt CLI standard: complete product surface on CLI
description:
Adopt punt-kit CLI standard: the CLI is the complete product. Every MCP tool and slash command has a corresponding CLI command. The CLI is the source of truth; MCP tools and slash commands project it.

## Core principle (from standard)

"A user who never opens Claude Code can use every feature."

Currently vox is inverted — the MCP server is the product and the CLI is secondary. This bead inverts that dependency: implement in CLI first, project to MCP/plugin.

## Current state (post vox-k0o)

CLI commands: `synthesize`, `synthesize-batch`, `synthesize-pair`, `synthesize-pair-batch`, `doctor`, `install`, `uninstall`, `install-desktop`, `play`, `mcp`

MCP tools: `speak`, `chorus`, `duet`, `ensemble`, `set_config`, `list_voices`

Slash commands: `/say`, `/recap`, `/notify`, `/speak`, `/vibe`, `/voice`

### Name mapping (important — names differ across surfaces)

| CLI (proposed) | MCP tool | Slash command | Notes |
|----------------|----------|---------------|-------|
| `say` | `speak` | `/say`, `/recap` | MCP is `speak`, slash is `/say`. Different names. |
| `notify` | `set_config` | `/notify` | Config mutation, not its own MCP tool |
| `speak` | `set_config` | `/speak` | **Name collision**: slash `/speak` toggles voice-vs-chime; MCP `speak` synthesizes audio |
| `vibe` | `set_config` | `/vibe` | Config mutation |
| `voice` | `set_config` + `list_voices` | `/voice` | `/voice` (no arg) calls `list_voices` for browsing; `/voice <name>` calls `set_config` |
| `chorus` | `chorus` | — | No slash command exists |
| `duet` | `duet` | — | No slash command exists |
| `ensemble` | `ensemble` | — | No slash command exists |

## Product commands needed (Layer 1)

### New commands — projecting existing MCP tools to CLI

- [ ] `say <text>` — synthesize + auto-play + ephemeral cleanup. Maps to MCP `speak` tool. This is NOT a rename of `synthesize` — `synthesize` writes a persistent file, `say` plays and discards.
- [ ] `chorus <texts...>` — multi-voice synthesis. Maps to MCP `chorus` tool.
- [ ] `duet <text1> <text2>` — paired bilingual synthesis. Maps to MCP `duet` tool.
- [ ] `ensemble <manifest>` — multi-voice batch from JSON manifest. Maps to MCP `ensemble` tool.

### New commands — projecting existing slash commands to CLI

These slash commands call `set_config` MCP tool. CLI equivalents read/write `.vox/config.md` directly (no MCP dependency).

- [ ] `notify y|c|n` — toggle task notifications (maps to `/notify`)
- [ ] `speak y|n` — toggle voice vs chime delivery (maps to `/speak`)
- [ ] `vibe <mood>|auto|off` — set session mood (maps to `/vibe`)
- [ ] `voice [on|off|status|<name>|clear]` — voice mode and session voice (maps to `/voice`). With no arg, lists available voices (subsumes the `list_voices` MCP tool — no separate `voices` command needed).

## Admin commands needed (Layer 2)

- [ ] `version` — output: `vox <semver>` (typer supports `--version` callback)
- [ ] `status` — current provider, voice, vibe, config state

## Existing commands that stay

- `synthesize` — persistent file output (not replaced by `say`)
- `synthesize-batch`, `synthesize-pair`, `synthesize-pair-batch` — batch variants
- `doctor`, `install`, `play`, `mcp` — keep as-is

## Existing commands to retire

- [ ] `install-desktop` — fold into `install` (subcommand or flag)
- [ ] `uninstall` — fold into `install --uninstall` or keep if cleaner

## README updates

The README CLI section is stale post vox-k0o:
- [ ] `vox serve` → `vox mcp`
- [ ] Add new product commands once implemented
- [ ] Document `say` vs `synthesize` distinction

## Projection alignment

After CLI commands exist, verify:
- MCP tools and CLI commands call the same core functions
- Slash commands instruct Claude to call the corresponding MCP tool
- No capability exists in plugin that CLI cannot do
- No capability exists in CLI that plugin cannot access

Depends on vox-k0o (completed — PR #59 merged).
close_reason: Shipped in PR #60 (mic API redesign)

title: elevenlabs SDK pydantic v1 warning on Python 3.14
description:
When running vox via uv tool install on Python 3.14, elevenlabs SDK emits:

```
elevenlabs/core/pydantic_utilities.py:13: UserWarning: Core Pydantic V1 functionality isn't compatible with Python 3.14 or greater.
  from pydantic.v1.datetime_parse import parse_date as parse_date
```

This is upstream in the elevenlabs Python SDK — they import pydantic v1 compat layer which is incompatible with 3.14. Not actionable on our side until elevenlabs ships a fix. Track to monitor and suppress the warning if it becomes user-facing noise.

Seen in: /Users/jfreeman/.local/share/uv/tools/punt-vox/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elevenlabs/core/pydantic_utilities.py:13
close_reason: Suppressed via warnings.filterwarnings in providers/__init__.py and pytest filterwarnings in pyproject.toml (PR #71)

title: Root-cause flaky watcher notification consumer tests on CI
description:
Two watcher tests skip on CI (Ubuntu) but pass locally (macOS): test_throttles_same_signal and test_different_signals_not_throttled. The _announce_voice mock call_count is 0 when expected 1+.

## Hypothesis
The consumer function (make_notification_consumer) reads config from tmp_path and gates on config.notify != 'c' at line 199 before reaching the mocked _announce_voice. The config YAML is written as '---\nnotify: "c"\nspeak: "y"\n---\n'. On Ubuntu CI, the config read may return a different default (e.g. notify='n') causing an early return before the mock is hit. This would explain why call_count is always 0 regardless of test logic.

## Next step
Re-run CI to check if the flakiness still reproduces after recent changes. If it does, add debug logging to print config.notify on CI before investigating further.

title: Bug: vibe_signals grows unbounded in long sessions
description:
Z spec audit finding: vibe_signals in .vox/config.md grows unbounded. Each Bash command appends a timestamped token. In long sessions this becomes hundreds of entries on a single YAML line.

## Current State
handle_post_bash() appends 'signal@HH:MM' to vibe_signals without pruning. The Z spec bounds this with maxSignals.

## Fix
Prune vibe_signals to keep only the last N signals (e.g., 20) or signals from the current hour. The vibe resolver only needs recent trajectory, not the full history.

## Implementation
In handle_post_bash() after appending:
1. Split signals by comma
2. If len > MAX_SIGNALS (e.g., 20): keep only the last MAX_SIGNALS
3. Write back trimmed string

## Z Spec Reference
docs/claude-code-vox.tex, State invariant: # vibeSignals <= maxSignals
close_reason: Fixed in PR #91: vibe_signals pruned to 20 entries

title: Spell out acronyms like OCR as individual letters before synthesis
description:
## Problem

Vox tries to pronounce uppercase acronyms as words instead of spelling
them out. OCR becomes "ocker," MCP becomes "mick-pee," TTS becomes
"tits," CLI becomes "clee," etc.

## Expected behavior

Uppercase letter sequences (2+ chars, not in a known-words dictionary)
should be spaced out so the TTS engine spells them:

- OCR -> "O C R"
- MCP -> "M C P"
- TTS -> "T T S"
- CLI -> "C L I"
- API -> "A P I" (or kept as-is if the provider pronounces it correctly)
- NATS -> "NATS" (real word, keep as-is)
- JSON -> provider-dependent (some say "jason" correctly)

## Proposed approach

Add acronym detection to the normalize_for_speech() pipeline from
vox-uvj. Heuristic: all-caps tokens of 2-5 chars that aren't in a
known-pronounceable set get letter-spaced. Maintain a small allowlist
of acronyms that TTS engines handle correctly (JSON, SQL, NATS, AWS, etc).

## Depends on

vox-uvj (normalize_for_speech pipeline)
close_reason: Merged PR #144 — acronym spacing with ~280-entry pronounceable allowlist

title: Architecture B: log-tailing MCP server for /notify c continuous mode
description:
Session event watcher: background thread in the MCP server that tails the active session JSONL. Foundational infrastructure for two consumers:

1. **Continuous notifications** (notify=c): announces milestones via speech — no model involvement, no extra tokens. Detects git commits, test completions, new files, subagent completions by pattern matching.

2. **Auto-vibe signals** (tts-5eo): feeds richer context to mood detection — model frustration patterns, user tone, error density over time, task arcs. Far richer than the current PostToolUse-only signal window.

Key design decisions:
1. Session discovery: find active JSONL by most-recently-modified in project dir
2. Throttle: minimum N seconds between announcements
3. Consumer architecture: single watcher thread, multiple consumers (notifications + mood scoring)
4. Config: reads .tts/config.md — notifications only active when notify=c, mood scoring only when vibe_mode=auto
5. Audio collision: must coordinate with stop hook and model-driven speak
6. Synthesis: uses existing provider pipeline
7. Respects speak=n chime mode

Complementary to tts-1p3 (screen reader mode). Screen reader fires synchronously after tool calls via hooks. The watcher fires asynchronously during long-running operations when the model is busy and hooks can't fire.

Current state: notify=c is accepted but behaves identically to notify=y. This bead builds the watcher and the notification consumer.
close_reason: Closed

title: Hook: UserPromptSubmit acknowledgment in continuous mode
description:
Z spec enhancement: Add async UserPromptSubmit hook that speaks a short acknowledgment in continuous mode.

## Behavior
- Only fires when notifyMode = 'c' (continuous)
- Async (fire-and-forget, does not block prompt processing)
- Speaks a random short phrase: 'On it!', 'Consider it done.', 'Right away.', 'Let me take a look.', etc.
- If speak=n: play acknowledgment chime instead

## Implementation
1. Add UserPromptSubmit section to hooks/hooks.json (async=true, no matcher)
2. Create hooks/acknowledge.sh dispatcher (or reuse shared dispatcher pattern)
3. Add handle_user_prompt_submit() to hooks.py:
   - Check config.notify == 'c', else return
   - Pick random phrase from ACKNOWLEDGE_PHRASES
   - Call vox unmute or enqueue chime
4. Add ACKNOWLEDGE_PHRASES pool (5-7 phrases)

## Z Spec Reference
docs/claude-code-vox.tex, Section 10 (Continuous Mode Enhancements), AcknowledgePrompt operation.
Modeled as Xi State (no state change) — pure async side effect.
close_reason: Merged PR #86

title: Auto-vibe: detect session mood from hook signals
description:
LLM-based transcript mood analysis for richer auto-vibe signals.

## What's already done
- vibe_mode config (auto/manual/off) fully wired across CLI, MCP, hooks
- PostToolUse signal accumulation (classify_signal → vibe_signals in config)
- resolve_tags_from_signals() deterministic mapping to ElevenLabs tags
- Stop hook reads vibe_mode, applies tags in auto mode, respects manual override
- JSONL watcher tails session transcript, classifies bash output, dispatches events

## Remaining scope
The watcher currently does the same regex pattern matching as the hooks. The enhancement is to use the watcher's richer context (full transcript access) for mood signals that regex can't detect:
- Model frustration patterns (repeated retries on the same error)
- Error density over time (escalating vs resolving)
- Task arc detection (debugging → breakthrough → cleanup)
- Session trajectory (productive vs stuck)

This would supplement the existing deterministic signal→tag mapping with a second, context-aware signal source. The deterministic path remains the default; transcript analysis would refine it.
close_reason: Deterministic signal mapping is sufficient; LLM-based transcript analysis not needed

title: Live microphone capture for voice-to-agent conversation
description:
Live voice conversation between user and Claude via ElevenLabs Conversational AI API.

## ElevenLabs Conversational AI
ElevenLabs offers a full-duplex Conversational AI platform (not half-duplex turn-taking):
- WebSocket API with sub-100ms latency
- Handles VAD, interruption, overlap — feels like a real conversation
- LLM-integrated: supports Claude as the backend brain
- Python SDK available (same elevenlabs package we already use)
- Multimodal: voice + text simultaneously

## Architecture
This is NOT a traditional STT → LLM → TTS pipeline. The ElevenLabs Conversational AI API handles the entire loop as a single WebSocket connection. Vox would be a thin wrapper:
1. Open WebSocket to ElevenLabs Conversational AI
2. Configure Claude as the LLM backend
3. Stream microphone audio in, get voice audio out
4. ElevenLabs handles VAD, transcription, synthesis, and interruption

## Implications
- May NOT need vox-9b3 (standalone STT) as a dependency — ElevenLabs bundles STT internally
- Provider fallback (Polly, OpenAI) does not apply — this is an ElevenLabs-specific feature

title: Audio queue: play utterances sequentially instead of kill-previous
description:
The kill-previous fix (267c700) prevents overlapping audio but discards the interrupted utterance. All three playback paths (MCP _play_audio, stop hook chime, notification hook chime/voice) should enqueue audio instead, so every utterance plays to completion in FIFO order.

Possible approaches:
- A lightweight daemon/socket that accepts file paths and plays them sequentially
- A named pipe or Unix domain socket that all three paths write to
- A lock file with a wait loop (simplest but blocks the caller)

The queue must work across Python (server.py) and bash (hooks) and across independent Claude Code sessions.
close_reason: Implemented in v0.4.0 via flock-based playback serialization (playback.py). enqueue() + play_audio() provide FIFO ordering across Python and bash callers.

title: Investigate duplicate audio playback (3-5x repeats)

Initially observed with voice 'george', switched voices to test — problem persists. NOT voice-specific. This is a systemic ElevenLabs API issue. Next steps: (1) check ElevenLabs status page for known issues, (2) test with a different model (e.g. turbo v2.5 vs multilingual v2), (3) consider filing an ElevenLabs support ticket with the dashboard evidence.

title: Design command for notify-only mode (notify: y without voice)
description:
Implement the notify state machine redesign specified in docs/vox-notify.tex (Z spec, ProB verified).

**Design** (formally verified: 19 states, 170 transitions, no deadlocks):

Two independent axes with clean separation:
- **Notify axis** (`/vox y|n|c`): controls which events trigger feedback
- **Speak axis** (`/unmute` / `/mute`): controls voice vs chime output

Key behaviors:
1. `SpeakMode` has 3 values: `sUnset | sChime | sVoice`
2. `/vox y` and `/vox c` initialize speak to `sVoice` only when `sUnset` (first activation)
3. Once user explicitly sets speak via `/mute` or `/unmute`, that choice is sticky across notify changes
4. `/unmute` no longer forces `notify=c` — it only sets `speak=sVoice`

**Code changes needed**:
1. `server.py` `notify()` tool: remove `speak=y` force on `mode=c`; only set speak when config has no speak field yet
2. `server.py` `notify()` tool: `mode=y` should also init speak if unset (currently only first-init does this)
3. `commands/unmute.md`: remove `mode=c` from notify call — unmute should only call `speak` tool
4. `config.py`: speak default changes from `y` to empty/unset for new configs
5. Tests: update test_hooks.py, test_server.py for new state transitions

**Files**: server.py, config.py, commands/unmute.md, commands/vox.md, hooks.py, watcher.py, tests/
close_reason: PR #81 merged — two-axis state machine with Z spec

title: Vibe-driven chime notifications (non-speak mode)
description:
When speak=n, all notifications currently play the same generic chime_done.mp3 or chime_prompt.mp3 regardless of session mood. This feature makes chime-only notifications vibe-aware.

Current state:
- assets/chime_done.mp3 — single task-complete chime
- assets/chime_prompt.mp3 — single permission-prompt chime
- Stop hook (notify.sh) plays chime_done.mp3 when speak=n
- Notification hook (notify-permission.sh) plays chime_prompt.mp3
- Watcher (_announce_chime) plays chime_done.mp3 for all signals

Desired behavior:
- Chime selection varies based on current vibe_tags/vibe mood from .tts/config.md
- Different tones for different moods (warm/happy → gentle major-key chime, focused → crisp minimal tone, tense → alert tone, etc.)
- Different tones for different signal types in watcher mode (tests-pass vs tests-fail vs git-push-ok)
- Could use pre-generated chime variants (asset library) or dynamically generate short clips via ElevenLabs Music API (depends on tts-0qi)

Design considerations:
- Pre-generated assets: fast, no API cost, finite palette — good for v1
- Dynamic generation: infinite variety, matches vibe exactly, but adds latency and API cost
- Hybrid: small asset library for common moods, fallback to generation for rare combinations
- Chime selection logic lives in playback path (hooks and watcher), reads vibe from config
- Signal-type differentiation: success signals get positive tones, failure signals get alert tones, independent of vibe

title: Support dev-only and dev+prod dual plugin environments
description:
Currently running `claude --plugin-dir .` loads the dev plugin (tts-dev) alongside the installed prod plugin (tts). This dual-env causes confusing behavior — e.g. dev MCP tool calls can route through the prod `tts serve` binary, which runs older code without recent fixes (observed: _apply_vibe double-tag prepend because prod lacks PR #27 fix).

Two environments to support:
1. **Dev-only**: only the working tree plugin is active, using the editable install binary. No prod plugin interference.
2. **Dev+prod dual**: both namespaces active simultaneously (current behavior). Useful for comparing prod vs dev behavior.

Needs investigation into how to cleanly isolate the dev environment when desired — potentially a flag, env var, or workflow change. The key problem is that `tts serve` in plugin.json resolves to whichever `tts` binary is on PATH, which may be the prod install rather than the editable install.

title: Config path resolution assumes git for standalone pip users
description: resolve_config_path() shells out to git rev-parse on every CLI config command (notify, speak, voice, vibe, status). For Claude Code this is correct — it finds the git root and shares config across worktrees. But for standalone pip install users (no git repo, possibly no git binary), it spawns a wasted subprocess that always fails before falling back to CWD-relative DEFAULT_CONFIG_PATH. Options: (1) walk up dirs looking for .vox/ without git, (2) check for git cheaply before shelling out, (3) accept the overhead since the fallback works. The Claude Code case is fully correct today — this is only about the standalone CLI experience.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #80: added @functools.lru_cache(maxsize=1) to resolve_config_path(). One git subprocess per session instead of dozens.

title: Streaming TTS: use ElevenLabs stream API to reduce time-to-first-audio
description: Replace batch text_to_speech.convert() with text_to_speech.stream() for the speak MCP tool. The text is already known upfront so this is HTTP streaming (not WebSocket), but audio chunks start playing before full synthesis completes. Reduces perceived latency from ~1-2s to ~75ms with Flash v2.5 model. Does not change the API surface — callers still get an MP3 file back. The stream() method returns Iterator[bytes] which we write to disk as chunks arrive, then play. Optional: pipe chunks directly to afplay via stdin for true streaming playback (no temp file). This is a latency optimization, not a feature change.
close_reason: Released in v0.3.6

title: Auto-add ElevenLabs community voices to user library via API
description:
Auto-add ElevenLabs community voices to user library on first use.

**Current behavior**: `_resolve_voice_id()` fetches the user's voice library, and if the name isn't found, raises `VoiceNotFoundError`. Community/shared voices not yet added to the library are invisible.

**Desired behavior**: When `_resolve_voice_id()` fails to find a voice in the user's library, search the ElevenLabs shared voice catalog (GET `/v1/shared-voices`) for a match. If found, auto-add it to the user's library (POST `/v1/voices/add/{public_user_id}/{voice_id}`), refresh the local cache, and retry resolution.

**Scope**:
1. Add `_search_shared_voices(name)` → searches ElevenLabs shared voice catalog by name
2. Add `_add_shared_voice(public_user_id, voice_id)` → adds voice to user library
3. Modify `_resolve_voice_id()`: after cache miss, before raising VoiceNotFoundError, try shared voice search + auto-add + cache refresh

title: Vox applet for Lux
description:
## Overview

Display a persistent \"Vox\" widget in the Lux window that shows current
Vox state and allows interactive control. The widget should appear
automatically when Lux is available and stay present throughout the session,
updating as Vox state changes.

## Current Design (prototype working)

Frame title: "Vox"

Layout (vertical stack):
1. **Notifications** — radio: Off | On | Continuous (maps to notify mode n/y/c)
2. **Voice** — radio: Mute | Unmute (maps to speak mode n/y)
3. **At the Mic** — combo: charlie, lily, jessica, roger, matilda, sarah
4. **Vibe** — combo: auto, chill, excited, serious, warm, off
5. **Engine** — read-only text showing current provider (e.g. "Engine:  ElevenLabs")

## Behavior

- Widget appears on session start when Lux display_mode is "on"
- State reflects live Vox config (notify, speak, voice, vibe, provider)
- Interactive controls wired via recv() to call Vox MCP tools:
  - Notifications radio → notify(mode=...)
  - Voice radio → speak(mode=...)
  - At the Mic combo → speak(mode="y", voice=...)
  - Vibe combo → vibe(mood=...)
- State updates flow back to widget via update()

## Blockers

- **lux#52**: frame_size and frame_flags not yet implemented — widget
  currently auto-sizes to content but frame fills available space.
  Once lux ships sizing, constrain to compact dimensions (~320x240).
- **window element crash**: the window kind element crashes Lux 0.9.0
  on macOS — cannot use as workaround for frame sizing.

## Implementation Notes

- Scene ID: "vox-studio"
- Frame ID: "vox-booth"  
- Voice roster should be dynamic (fetched from who() tool)
- Provider is info-only (no combo needed — switching provider mid-session is rare)
close_reason: Merged in PR #90: show_vox MCP tool + applet.py module

title: bug: AskUserQuestion dialog does not render inside skill/command execution
description:
When AskUserQuestion is called during a /voice skill execution, the dialog never appears. The tool returns immediately with empty answers. AskUserQuestion works correctly when called outside skill context (tested and confirmed). Suppress-output hook only matches MCP tools so it shouldn't interfere, but the interaction hasn't been ruled out.

## Investigation plan
1. Test AskUserQuestion inside a command with and without the suppress-output hook active
2. Test AskUserQuestion as the terminal action of a skill (continuation style)

## Potential fix: continuation-passing style
Structure the command so list_voices + formatting happens first, then AskUserQuestion is the very last action (the skill 'exits with' the dialog). When the user answers, the model already has context and completes the set_config call. This works if the issue is mid-skill rendering, not terminal-action rendering.

title: MP3 cache for quip phrases
description:
## Problem

Every hook invocation re-synthesizes the same short quip phrases via the TTS API. There are ~70 quips across 8 event pools (stop, permission, idle, pre-compact, acknowledge, subagent-start, subagent-stop, farewell). For users on metered providers (ElevenLabs, OpenAI), this burns API quota and adds latency on phrases that produce identical audio every time.

## Scope

### Architecture

Quips are fully centralized in `quips.py` — 8 immutable tuples, ~70 total phrases. All synthesis flows through two paths in `hooks.py`:
- `_speak_phrase()` — used by pre-compact, acknowledge, subagent-start, subagent-stop, farewell, session-end
- `handle_notification()` — used by permission/idle prompts

Both call `vox unmute` via subprocess. The MCP `unmute` tool in `server.py` already has a file-existence check (`if out_path.exists(): return`) but ephemeral mode cleans up after each call, defeating any natural caching.

### Cache key

`(text, voice, provider)` — same phrase + same voice + same provider = identical audio. Vibe tags affect the voice style but quip hooks don't pass vibe tags, so they aren't part of the key for this scope.

### Design constraints

- Cache must be voice-scoped: switching voices invalidates cached entries for that voice
- Cache location: `~/.punt-vox/cache/` (persistent across sessions, survives ephemeral cleanup)
- Cache should be bounded (LRU or size-capped) to prevent unbounded disk growth
- Pre-warming is a future optimization, not in scope — cache populates on first use
- Only quip phrases should be cached, not arbitrary `unmute` calls (user-generated text varies too much)
- Provider must be part of the key (same text on ElevenLabs vs Polly produces different audio)

### Implementation approach

1. Add a `cache.py` module with `get_cached(text, voice, provider) -> Path | None` and `put_cached(text, voice, provider, audio_path: Path) -> Path`
2. Cache key: hash of `(text, voice, provider_name)` -> `~/.punt-vox/cache/{hash}.mp3`
3. Integrate at the `_speak_phrase()` / `handle_notification()` level — check cache before calling `vox unmute`, play cached file directly if hit
4. Add `vox cache status` / `vox cache clear` CLI commands for user control
5. Tests: cache hit/miss, voice mismatch, cache clear

### What's NOT in scope

- Pre-warming the cache on install or first run
- Caching arbitrary `unmute`/`record` calls
- Network-based or shared caching
- Cache invalidation on provider version changes (manual `vox cache clear` suffices)
close_reason: Merged in PR #94 (commit 97340bf)

title: Design proper hook kill-switch mechanism
description:
The hooks currently have no way to be globally disabled short of editing each shell script. An emergency kill-switch (~/.punt-hooks-kill sentinel file) was prototyped but never merged. Design a proper mechanism: could be a sentinel file, a config field, a CLI command (vox hooks disable/enable), or integration with the plugin system. Should be a single toggle that disables all Vox hooks without uninstalling the plugin.

## Dependency: .punt-labs/ directory standardization

This should land alongside the org-wide migration from per-project dot directories (.vox/, .tts/, etc.) to a unified .punt-labs/{project}/ structure. The kill-switch config would live at .punt-labs/vox/ rather than .vox/. This is an org-level standards change tracked in punt-kit -- do not implement until that standard is written and adopted.

Current state: vox uses .vox/config.md for session state. Migration path: .vox/ -> .punt-labs/vox/.

title: Adopt CLI global flag standard (--quiet, mutual exclusion)
description:
## Context

Audit against punt-kit/standards/cli.md §Global Flags found 2 gaps. Vox is mostly compliant — it already has `_emit()`, correct flag ordering for existing flags, and no argv hoisting. Two changes bring it to full compliance.

## What's correct

- **Flag ordering**: `--json` before `--verbose/-v` (src/punt_vox/__main__.py:178-186)
- **No argv hoisting**: Only sys.argv use is in the isolated playback subprocess (playback.py:102), not the main CLI
- **`_emit()` adapter**: Centralized at lines 57-64, used by most commands
- **no_args_is_help=True**: Functionally equivalent to invoke_without_command

## What needs to change

### 1. Add `--quiet/-q` flag
**File:** `src/punt_vox/__main__.py:97-100`
- Add `Quiet` type alias alongside existing `Verbose` type alias
- Add `quiet: Quiet = False` parameter to `_callback()` after `verbose`
- Store in module-level `_quiet_output` global (matches existing `_json_output` pattern)
- Wire into `_emit()`: suppress text output when quiet and not JSON mode

### 2. Add mutual exclusion validation
**File:** `src/punt_vox/__main__.py:178-186` (in `_callback()`)
- Add after line 186:
  ```python
  if verbose and quiet:
      raise typer.BadParameter("--verbose and --quiet are mutually exclusive.")
  ```

### Optional: consolidate inline JSON checks
`status_cmd()` (lines 560-574) and `doctor()` (lines 727-731) have inline `if _json_output:` checks instead of using `_emit()`. Not blocking, but inconsistent with the rest of the CLI.

## Reference implementations
- **quarry** `_emit()` pattern: `quarry/src/quarry/__main__.py:102-113`
- **biff** mutual exclusion: `biff/src/biff/__main__.py:115-116`
close_reason: Added --quiet/-q flag, mutual exclusion with --verbose, _emit() suppresses text in quiet mode.

title: Translate programmer strings to natural speech before synthesis
description:
## Problem

Vox speaks programmer-facing strings verbatim, producing awkward audio.
Examples:

- eof_received -> speaks "eof underscore received" instead of "EOF received"
- snake_case identifiers spoken with "underscore" between words
- camelCase identifiers spoken as one word
- Abbreviations like stderr, stdout, stdin not expanded
- File paths like /usr/local/bin spoken literally

## Expected behavior

A text normalization pass before synthesis that converts programmer
strings to natural speech:

- snake_case -> space-separated words (eof_received -> "EOF received")
- camelCase -> space-separated words (fileName -> "file name")
- Known abbreviations expanded (stderr -> "standard error")
- Underscores between words become spaces
- ALL_CAPS preserved as spoken acronyms or expanded if known

## Proposed approach

Add a normalize_for_speech() function in the synthesis pipeline,
called after text is received but before provider API call. Should be
a lightweight string transform, not an LLM call.

## Scope

- Core library function in punt_vox
- Applied in speak/chorus/duet/ensemble paths
- Unit tests for common patterns
- Should not affect text written to file metadata (only audio output)
close_reason: Merged PR #93: normalize_for_speech() pipeline

title: PostToolUse hook matcher doesn't match standalone MCP tool names
description:
TTS plugin's PostToolUse suppress-output hook only fires when tts is loaded as a plugin, not as a standalone MCP server.

## Root Cause

The matcher in hooks.json is:
```
"matcher": "mcp__plugin_tts(-dev)?_vox__.*"
```

This matches plugin-namespaced tool names (`mcp__plugin_tts_vox__speak`, `mcp__plugin_tts-dev_vox__speak`) but NOT standalone MCP server names (`mcp__tts_vox__speak` or `mcp__vox__speak`).

Less likely to hit than quarry (tts is typically loaded as a plugin), but the same class of bug. If someone adds tts to .mcp.json as a standalone server, suppress-output won't fire.

## Fix

Update the matcher to handle both patterns:
```json
"matcher": "mcp__(plugin_tts(-dev)?_)?vox__.*"
```

This matches:
- `mcp__vox__*` (standalone)
- `mcp__plugin_tts_vox__*` (installed plugin)
- `mcp__plugin_tts-dev_vox__*` (dev plugin)

## Cross-repo

Same bug exists in quarry (quarry-fsx) and biff. Pattern fix is identical across all three.
close_reason: Closed

title: Add vibe_tags parameter to speak tool
description:
The stop-hook flow currently requires two tool calls: set_config (to apply vibe tags and clear signals) then speak. This produces two ♪ lines in the panel for what should be one action.

Add an optional vibe_tags parameter to the speak and chorus MCP tools. When provided, the tool applies the tags and clears vibe_signals internally before synthesis — no separate set_config needed.

Before (2 calls, 2 panel lines):
  set_config({vibe_tags: "[warm] [satisfied]", vibe_signals: ""})
  → ♪ vibe shifted to [warm] [satisfied]
  speak(text="All done.", ephemeral=true)
  → ♪ matilda has spoken

After (1 call, 1 panel line):
  speak(text="All done.", ephemeral=true, vibe_tags="[warm] [satisfied]")
  → ♪ matilda has spoken

Examples for permission_prompt: 'Hey, quick approval needed.', 'Need a green light here.', 'Got a question for you.', 'Your call on this one.' The variations should sound human and conversational, not corporate. Use bash RANDOM or shuf for selection. Keep the pool in the script itself (no external config).
